topic.what: GAMMAF benchmarking platform for graph-based anomaly detection in LLM multi-agent systems
topic.how: Generates synthetic multi-agent debate data across varied topologies and dynamically evaluates defense models through iterative rounds with adversarial node pruning
topic.why_matters: Addresses lack of standardized reproducible environment for training and evaluating graph-based anomaly detection defenses in LLM-MAS, enabling trustworthy comparison across methods

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of collective providing incorrect solutions under adversarial influence
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline no-defense ASR ~39-45%; defenses reduce to 5-28% @3
characteristics.context: @3 round shows substantial ASR reduction from early rounds

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recall of defense system; percentage of adversarial agents correctly identified
characteristics.vs_baseline: No-defense baseline has no detection capability (N/A)
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 showing ADR improvement from @1 to @3
characteristics.context: XG-Guard outperforms BlindGuard; @3 performance much higher than @1

characteristics.what_itasures: Proportion of trusted agents that are malfunctioning (stealthy attacks bypassing defense)
characteristics.context: XG-Guard shows lower stealthy attack rates than BlindGuard

characteristics.what_it_measures: Discrimination ability of defense model between benign and adversarial nodes
characteristics.context: Strong improvement from @1 to @3 across all methods

characteristics.what_it_measures: Speed of benchmarking process using concurrent inference
characteristics.value: Batch concurrent topology and task execution
characteristics.evidence: Figure 4 and concurrent batching described in Section 3
characteristics.context: vLLM serves backbone LLM with KV cache sharing across requests

characteristics.what_it_measures: Framework performance across different network topologies (Chain, Tree, Random, Star)
characteristics.value: Consistent metrics across Star, Tree, Chain, Random topologies
characteristics.context: Framework tested on multiple topological configurations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Token and inference cost savings from early defense pruning
characteristics.value: Early consensus and adversarial agent pruning reduces token generation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Undefended MAS consumes full token budget
characteristics.context: Defensive pruning cuts off extensive token generation by adversarial agents

good_for: evaluating graph-based anomaly detection in LLM-MAS
benchmarking defense mechanisms against prompt injection
researching attack propagation and infection dynamics
requires: LLM backbone for agent inference (gpt-oss-20b or equivalent)

novelty: First open-source benchmarking platform for LLM-MAS anomaly detection
Dual-pipeline architecture decoupling training data generation from dynamic defense evaluation
Dynamic topology updating through adversarial node pruning during live inference rounds
Isolation of agent reasoning from final categorical response in embeddings to prevent answer-based overfitting
open_problems: Generalization to novel attack patterns not seen during training
Extension to multi-hop attacks and coordinated adversarial coalitions
Benchmarking against more diverse defense architectures beyond XG-Guard and BlindGuard
Scalability to larger MAS with hundreds of agents
Integration of external tools and retrievable memory attack scenarios
